title: Government contractors
description: Breakthrough R&D demands the highest caliber skills in science, engineering, communications, compliance, contracting, and more. Answer the call.
text:
Program launches, challenges, commercial transition activities â DARPAâs work pushes the edge of the possible.
To make it happen, our program managers need highly skilled support contractors, broadly referred to as SETAs (Scientific Engineering and Technical Assistance) within DARPA. Often spanning programs, portfolios, and even offices in their impact, support contractors help program managers chart and navigate the path toward groundbreaking discoveries.
Enable the Impossible
Support contractor professionals are part of the DARPA team and are indispensable partners to the Government, contributing to the trajectory of ambitious research efforts from conceptualization to execution. Our contract partners recruit exceptional professionals with critical expertise who are deeply curious, intellectually agile, and committed to advancing DARPAâs mission.
From financial analysts and contract specialists to scientists and subject matter experts in the most groundbreaking technology, there could be a role for you at DARPA.
We rely on several companies to fill support contractor roles within our technical and support offices. If you know the DARPA office in which youâd like to work, click on the company(/ies) below that currently hold contracts supporting them to find open opportunities.
Please keep checking back as these sites are regularly updated with new DARPA support contractor positions to fulfill exciting emergent needs that arise. If youâre not sure where your skills and interests apply, learn more about each technical and support offices. | Learn more about our offices
Contractor names and links are provided for convenience. The listing does not constitute an endorsement of the contractor or its services by DARPA or the Department of War (DOW), nor does it create an employer-employee relationship between DARPA and contractor personnel.
Companies and their personnel may only be a research and development performer or support contractor, not both, in accordance with Organizational Conflict of Interest restrictions.
